pages.full_text:
Mark Hammond are sworn rivals on the village cricket teams which share the same field. We had more coffee.

The field is so boggy that we are camping on the carpark. John's course finishes at 4. + then the boys played football + ping pong.

__April 8th__
    Having packed John off to music, Peter + Charles + Eve went to pick up Robert to shop in Gloucester. He found a good carpark for us. Then we went bad for a long, comfortable lunch with Liz + Mark + Victoria + Louisa. Mark's father wins prizes with his wine.
    It started raining as we set off to Beauchamp, + was a wet evening. Simon joined us at 8.15. for a late supper.

__April 9th__
    We went to the Waterways Museum at Gloucester. It is very cold +
pages.notes: Page continues from previous spread mid-sentence ('Mark Hammond are sworn rivals...'). Year cannot be determined from these pages alone; volume covers 1989–2003. 'Beauchamp' likely refers to a campsite or location near Gloucester/Warwickshire. 'bad' in 'we went bad for a long, comfortable lunch' may be a misread or slip for 'back' — transcribed as written. The word before 'for a long' is unclear; could read 'bad' or 'back'. Text continues onto right page.

pages.full_text:
Inland Waterways by Alan Firth
    Opened in 1794 with the completion of the Warwick + B'ham Canal, Shrewley Tunnel on the Grand Union Canal is remarkable for the separate tunnel for the towpath [illegible] main tunn[el]

[glued-in postcard/illustration: Victorian-style colour print of a horse towing a narrowboat through a canal tunnel, man walking on towpath]

old +
photo_captions: Inland Waterways by Alan Firth — Opened in 1794 with the completion of the Warwick + B'ham Canal, Shrewley Tunnel on the Grand Union Canal is remarkable for the separate tunnel for the towpath [illegible] main tunn[el]
pages.notes: Handwritten page number '5' at top right — likely a trip-local page number rather than a volume page number. A colour postcard or illustration is glued in, depicting a horse-drawn narrowboat entering/exiting a canal tunnel in Victorian style. The handwritten text alongside the image appears to be a caption copied from a museum exhibit or book ('Inland Waterways by Alan Firth'), describing the Shrewley Tunnel on the Grand Union Canal. The text is written vertically/at an angle around the image and is partially obscured, making some words illegible. 'old +' at the bottom left is the continuation from the left page ('very cold +'), cut off at the gutter overlap.
